questionHtml:
0.50 mol of I2 (g) and 0.50 mol of Br2 (g) are placed in a closed flask. The following equilibrium is established.

I2 (g) + Br2 (g) ⇌ 2IBr (g)

The equilibrium mixture contains 0.80 mol of IBr (g). What is the value of Kc?

A.  0.64

B.  1.3

C.  2.6

D.  64

meta.title: 0.50 mol of I2 (g) and 0.50 mol of Br2 (g) are placed in a closed flask. The following equilibrium is established. I2 (g) + Br2 (g) ⇌ 2IBr (g) The equilibrium mixture contains 0.80
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 2. How much, how fast and how far?
Reactivity 2.3—How far? The extent of chemical change

Reactivity 2.3.6—The equilibrium law is the basis for quantifying the composition of an equilibrium mixture. Solve problems involving values of K and initial and equilibrium concentrations of the components of an equilibrium mixture.
